threads.subject: 🚨 CDI - Head of Data (H/F) 🚨
threads.body:
🚨 CDI - Head of Data (H/F) 🚨
🚨 CDI - Head of Data (H/F) 🚨

      Elio El Bitar
        Reply
        

Bonjour,

Mon client, réseau social qui permet d'explorer la diversité des pratiques sexuelles dans un cadre sécurisé, cherche un Head of Data (H/F) pour être garant de la cohérence de la vision data, de l'équipe data, et de la transformation des données en insights actionnables pour le produit.

.
💥 Le défi :

* Construire et piloter une roadmap data (architecture technique, IA, prédiction, optimisation produit)
* Réaliser un état des lieux complet de la donnée et la rendre accessible et exploitable par toutes les équipes
* Industrialiser et automatiser les analyses via Tableau, Quicksight, Amplitude, GSheet ou d'autres outils à déployer
* Manipuler et modéliser la donnée au quotidien (SQL, Python, AWS, ETL) avec un fort engagement opérationnel
* Accompagner les équipes internes dans l'adoption de la data et instaurer une culture de Data Driven Development
* Définir, fiabiliser et suivre les KPI business et produit
* Manager et faire monter en compétence l'équipe data


💻 Stack technique :

- Python / SQL
- Analyse de cohortes, funnels, A/B tests
- Data visualisation / BI (ex : Tableau, Quicksight, Amplitude)
- Maîtrise de la manipulation de données, y compris la collecte, l'organisation, la transformation, la visualisation et l'analyse de données


🎯 Votre profil :
* Expérience sur projets à fort trafic
* A l'aise avec l'anglais
* Facilité à vulgariser ses propos
* Connaissance globale du fonctionnement web
* A l'aise avec le management
* Force de proposition et fédérateur


💸 Salaire : 60K – 70K (selon expérience)
 💼 Contrat : CDI à temps plein
 📍 Lieu : Levallois / Télétravail (3 jours)

Si l'opportunité vous intéresse, n'hésitez pas à me communiquer vos coordonnées pour que nous puissions en discuter.

PS : Bien entendu si vous connaissez une autre personne susceptible d'être intéressé, je suis preneur ! 🙂

Bonne fin de journée et à bientôt !

Elio El Bitar
eelbitar@matchunt.com
🗣️  🇫🇷 🇬🇧 🇪🇸 🇱🇧

related_tasks.task_name: Writing: AI slot? Is it necessary bad? We are now generating huge amounts of data. It's not human vs ai, it's valuable vs noise. The key is no longer to get the info, it's to get the right info, at the right time, in the right form. It's to sort through everything available to know what's relevant.

GitHub Enterprise trial accounts are automatically deleted 90 days after the trial period ends.

threads.subject: 😟 Your Excalidraw+ subscription plan was canceled
threads.snippet: We're sorry to say goodbye, but we hope to see you soon on board again. Excalidraw+ Goodbye for now from Excalidraw+ Hey there, Your Excalidraw+ subscription plan was canceled. We're sorry to
threads.body:
We're sorry to say goodbye, but we hope to see you soon on board again.

Excalidraw+ (  )

*Goodbye for now from Excalidraw+*

Hey there,

Your Excalidraw+ subscription plan was canceled. We're sorry to say goodbye, but we hope to see you soon on board again. Our team is working on *amazing features* that *will only be available to Plus users* !

Don't forget to *follow our* *Twitter* (  ) *,* *LinkedIn* (  ) *or* *join our Discord* (  ) ** to ** stay up-to-date and not miss any of this exciting news!

Until then, *you can still enjoy the benefits of our forever-free editor*. Even with 1 scene, you can create outstanding results in our unique hand-drawn-like style that will take your projects to the next level.

Use free editor (  )

And – if you would like to share some thoughts with us, go ahead and drop us an email at *support@excalidraw.com* ( support@excalidraw.com?subject=Excalidraw+ feedback&body= ).

Thanks for being the part of Excalidraw+ community!

*With love,*
The Excalidraw+ team

(  ) (  ) (  )

Pourquoi avez-vous reçu un mail d'Avis Vérifiés ? Le Client d'Avis Vérifiés souhaite connaître votre expérience en tant que consommateur.rice d'un ou de plusieurs de ses produits que vous avez acheté(s) et/ou comme bénéficiaire d'un ou de plusieurs de ses services qu'il vous a délivré(s).  En savoir plus   sur la gestion de vos données et vos droits.
                                                                    Votre avis ainsi que toutes informations supplémentaires renseignées dans le formulaire seront publiés sur l'attestation du site concerné ainsi que sur le(s) widget(s) présents sur le site du client et plateformes tierces choisies par ce dernier. Seuls votre prénom et la première lettre de votre nom seront affichés avec votre avis. Pour plus de renseignements vous pouvez consulter notre  Politique de Confidentialité . directement  Bsp-auto.com. threads.subject: Re: Budapest Data+AI Forum 2026 - You are on the shortlist - Andriy Mandyev
threads.snippet: Hi Bence, Thanks for letting me know. Yes, that would be of interest - I'd be happy to deliver a virtual talk via Zoom on May 20th. Best, Andriy Andriy Mandyev On Mon, Apr 20, 2026 at 20:29 PM
threads.body:
Hi Andriy,

I'm sorry to inform you that we will not be able to grant you an in-person
speaking slot this time.

I apologize for the delay - we were trying to add more slots to accommodate
more speakers, but haven't succeeded due to constraints of the venue.

We do have a  few free slots for virtual/online talks, to be delivered via
Zoom on May 20th. (Wednesday).

Would that be of interest to you?

Bests,

Bence
Head of Program



*Budapest Data & AI Forum 2026*2026. May 18-20.
budapestdata.hu <https://budapestdata.hu/en/>

On Mon, Mar 30, 2026 at 10:35 AM Andriy Mandyev <a.mandyev@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Bence,
> Hope you had a good weekend. Any news about my submission?
> Thanks,
> Andriy
>
> On Tue, Mar 17, 2026 at 8:53 AM Andriy Mandyev <a.mandyev@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Bence,
>>
>> Thank you for taking interest in my submission! I'd be very excited to
>> share my thoughts at the Data+AI Forum.
>> Here's a link to book a slit with me to discuss this further:
>> https://calendar.app.google/PrzRzV9Bdba4Q3Fw5
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Andriy Mandyev
>>
>> On Mon, Mar 16, 2026, 18:49 Bence Arato <arato.bence@biconsulting.hu>
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Dear Speaker,
>>>
>>> Thank you very much for submitting a proposal to our Call for Speakers.
>>>
>>> I'm glad to inform you that your submission(s) has been favourably
>>> reviewed by the PMC and* made it to the shortlist*.
>>>
>>> As the next step, we would like to organize a short intro call with each
>>> shortlisted speaker, so we can get to know each other a bit better. These
>>> calls are typically 15-20 minutes long and attended by one or two PMC
>>> members from our side.
>>>
>>> We plan to do these intro calls in the next few days, so I'd like to ask
>>> you  to send us a few time slots when you would be available. These could
>>> be specific 30 minute slots or flexible ranges (e.g  "Wednesday 16:00-18:00
>>> or Thursday 10:00-12:00 is fine").  Please *do include* your location
>>> and time zone (e.g " I'm in New York on ET time").
>>>
>>> Looking forward to talk to you,
>>>
>>> Bence Arató
>>> Head of Program
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> *Budapest Data & AI Forum 2026*2026. May 18-20.
>>> budapestdata.hu <https://budapestdata.hu/en/>
>>>
>>
>
> --
> Andriy Mandyev

threads.subject: La Product Conf Paris 2026: Here's what you need to know before going on stage
threads.snippet: Dear Speakers, With La Product Conf 2026 on the horizon, we are thrilled to share this experience with you. Your participation is pivotal to the success of this event, and we extend our gratitude for
threads.body:
Dear Speakers,

With La Product Conf 2026 on the horizon, we are thrilled to share this
experience with you. Your participation is pivotal to the success of this
event, and we extend our gratitude for your commitment. Hugo and I,
alongside our Advisory Board, wanted to thank you very much for your
involvement and your presence on this event!

To ensure a smooth and organized event, *please read carefully* the
following guidelines and key dates. For more detailed information, I
encourage you to visit your speaker playbook
<
was previously shared with you.


*🧠 BEFORE LPC: THE PREPARATION*

   1. *Main Stage Speakers:* Submit the final version of your presentation
   slides to the designated *Google Drive folder
   <
    *by April 29*. The most updated version of the program is available on
   the LPC website
   <
   please refer to it to know your slots and timing. You may tweak your slides
   until the day before the event, but the final submission *must be in
   .ppt, .pptx or keynote format*. We cannot accept Google Slides.
   2. *Technical Requirements:* Personal computers are not permitted. All
   multimedia files (videos, fonts, gif animations…) should be uploaded to the
   same Google Drive folder. We cannot accommodate live streaming of YouTube
   or other internet content. No last-minute changes to your slides will be
   accepted on the d-day.
   3. *Rehearsal: Whether you are speaking on the main stage or the second
   stages (La Scène Parisienne 1 & 2),* please join us on May 18, between 4
   pm and 8 pm, for a *mandatory rehearsal.* You may book a slot in the
   provided *Google Sheet*
   <
   You will be talking in front of +1000 people, there's no room for
   improvisation. If you are speaking on a roundtable, you may also come on
   May 19 to rehearse or get a feel for the room.
   4. Please dress casually for the rehearsal gathering as some pictures of
   you will be taken while rehearsing.
   5. A few days before LPC, you will be contacted by one person of our
   staff (LPC buddy) who will help you navigate from the makeup station to the
   stage and to the common areas. They will get in touch with you via text or
   WhatsApp. *Your LPC buddy is here to:* make sure you are on time and
   ready to speak (make-up + equipped with a microphone) accompany you from
   the speaker's corner to the backstage. Please play the game and be
   respectful of them, we won't be chasing you throughout the day 😉
   6. Lastly, you are invited to the speaker's dinner after the rehearsals
   on the 19th. Please *register here*
   <
   to confirm your attendance.


*🎙️ DURING LPC: YOUR TALK*

   1. *Arrival on 19th May:*
      1. If you are speaking on the main stage, we kindly ask you to arrive
      by the entrance located 8 rue Saulnier 75009 Paris, at about 8am.
      2. If you are speaking in la Scène Parisienne, please enter from the
      entrance located on the right when you enter les Folies Bergère,
there will
      be a dedicated point for you to get your badge if you haven't had it the
      day before.
   2. Upon arrival, a volunteer will give you your badge and guide you to
   the speaker's corner where you will be able to relax, chill and rehearse if
   needed.
   3. *If you are speaking on the main stage*, please be in the speaker's
   corner at least *40 minutes before* your talk. Your chairperson will
   join you and a LPC buddy will come to fetch you to accompany you to the
   stage. *If you are speaking on a Product Conversation,* please meet in
   the speaker's corner of la Scène Parisienne at least *60 minutes
before* your
   talk. Your LPC Buddy will guide you, don't worry. Please make sure the
   participants of your talk are with you. You will get a little makeup
   touch-up and your microphone right after.


*☄ AFTER LPC: WHAT'S NEXT*

   1.

   *Content Sharing:* Your talk will be featured on our YouTube channel,
   LinkedIn, and Instagram account, reaching over 30,000 subscribers,
   approximately one month post-conference.

Should you have any inquiries or require further clarification, please do
not hesitate to reach out to me. 😊

Warm regards,
Grégoire Bonnin

Head of Marketing & Events
Thiga
[image: mobilePhone] 07 45 09 85 43
[image: emailAddress] gregoire.bonnin@thiga.co
[image: website] https://www.thiga.co/fr/
[image: address] 23, rue Taitbout, 75009 Paris

[image: linkedin]
<
instagram]
<

ᐧ

threads.subject: Generative AI Summit - more accurate and accountable AI output
threads.snippet: Open email for further resources Generative AI Summit London Hello Andriy, Hopefully you, like us, were left inspired by Generative AI Summit London last week. The event covered a range of AI topics,
threads.body:
<

Generative AI Summit London
Hello Andriy,

Hopefully you, like us, were left inspired by Generative AI Summit London last week.
The event covered a range of AI topics, including governance, responsible AI and the importance of AI-ready data. Graph databases, like those offered by Neo4j can help with all of these - helping provide context that results in more accurate and accountable output from AI.


We were there as an event sponsor - you may have spoken to one of our experts at the booth or joined our presentation on day one (Knowledge Graphs as a Reasoning Engine: Deploying Agents to Uncover Deep Insights).


Interested in learning more about how graph databases can support your AI projects? Check out some useful resources below:




	- Download the slides from our presentation here <
	- Have questions? Join our Ask the Expert session < on 27 May.
	- Want to speak to an expert? Get in touch <


We hope to see you at an event again soon!


Thanks,



Peter Deeley
Head of Marketing, UK&I
<    <    <    <
<
© 2026 Neo4j, Inc.


 Nordenskiöldsgatan 24, 211 19 Malmö, Sweden

threads.subject: Update on your ZurichJS Conference 2026 submission
threads.body:
Update on Your Submission

Hi Andriy,

Thank you for submitting "The Skills AI Won't Cover For You" to ZurichJS Conference 2026. We genuinely appreciate you taking the time to share your idea with us.

After careful review, we weren't able to include your submission in this year's program. This was a really hard decision for our committee.

Many of us on the program committee and organizing team are conference speakers ourselves. We know exactly how disheartening it feels to receive an email like this — we've been there, and we don't want you to feel that way. Your submission had merit, and the competition was simply fierce this year.

Some context on our selection

 * We received 434 submissions this year
 * Our committee completed 2489 reviews

threads.subject: LT#304 Learning from competition
threads.snippet: use your products deeply and as intended, not just for a quick look ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏
threads.body:
April 19, 2026 | Read Online
( https://leadershipintech.com/newsletters/2261?sid=97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003 )


Learning from competition
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22100/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

10 minutes by Steven Sinofsky

Studying competitors is essential for everyone on a product team,
not just managers or analysts. Steven suggests to use their
products deeply and as intended, not just for a quick look. Avoid
static thinking, since competitors know their own weaknesses and
will fix them. Tools like feature lists, scenario tests, and
writing mock reviews from a competitor's perspective all help
build real understanding.

New Survey: AI in Design and Development Workflows 2025
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22101/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

sponsored by Progress Telerik

84% of teams use AI—but many still struggle with consistency,
quality, and collaboration. Explore real adoption data, key
challenges, and what it takes to move from experimentation to
scalable workflows—plus what to watch next in 2026.

Why isn't everything different yet?
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22102/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

9 minutes by Dave Griffith

AI is transforming the economy, just not as fast as people
expect. Like electricity, the real productivity gains came
decades after the technology arrived, once workflows,
infrastructure, and business models caught up. Every domain needs
real people to figure out what changes and how. We are moving 5
to 10 times faster than past technology shifts, which still feels
slow because we are impatient.

How can I as a leader build hope in my team?
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22103/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

8 minutes by Andi Roberts

Hope is a practical leadership tool, not a soft sentiment. Gallup
research across 52 countries shows it is the top psychological
need employees have from their leaders, outweighing trust or
stability. Leaders build hope by giving teams clear goals,
multiple paths forward, and real ownership over their work.
Making small wins visible and being honest about uncertainty
keeps people believing their effort matters.

Blessed with constraints
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22104/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

9 minutes by Maarten Dalmijn

Too many choices overwhelm people just as much as too few.
Companies often pile on internal rules, approvals, and processes
that drain employee agency and create "railroading," where
everyone just keeps the machine running instead of solving real
problems. The fix is not removing all constraints but replacing
limiting ones with enabling ones like clear vision and strategy.
Maarten argues that when done right, this creates a sandbox where
teams can do their best work.

Why your best employee becomes your worst manager
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22105/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

5 minutes by Yaniv Preiss

Putting the wrong person in a management role hurts everyone: the
team disengages, the manager suffers, and trust in leadership
breaks down. The core mistake is treating management as a reward
for technical skill, when it is actually a new profession
requiring emotional intelligence, strategic thinking, and strong
communication.

Scale Your AI, Not Your Tech Debt: Vector Search Powered by Redis

( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22106/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

sponsored by Redis

Eliminate infrastructure sprawl by leveraging Redis as your
vector database. RedisVL lets your team deploy production-ready
RAG and semantic search on your existing footprint. No new
security reviews, no additional vendor contracts, just faster
time-to-market and lower TCO. Have your team run pip install
redisvl to turn your existing data into your next AI
breakthrough.

--------
industry
--------

* Cal.com Goes Closed Source
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22107/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

* Sometimes powerful people just do dumb things
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22108/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

* Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI
insiders and everyone else
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22109/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

* Protected quantum gates using qubit doublons in dynamical
optical lattices
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22110/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )


--------
security
--------

* Organizational Politics & The Security Program
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22111/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

* Cybersecurity Looks Like Proof of Work Now
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22112/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

* We May Be Living Through the Most Consequential Hundred Days in
Cyber History
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22113/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )

* Someone Bought 30 WordPress Plugins and Planted a Backdoor in
All of Them
( https://leadershipintech.com/links/22114/fd97e273-4ed4-4937-9b5d-7c7646830003/email )


And the most popular article from the last issue was:

threads.subject: Use Notetaker to improve your drafts
threads.snippet: Hi Andriy, Notetaker makes your drafts more accurate - if you talked about next steps on a call, your reply drafts will reflect that context. If you want better email drafts, connect your calendar in
threads.body:
Hi Andriy,

Notetaker makes your drafts more accurate - if you talked about next steps on a call, your reply drafts will reflect that context.

If you want better email drafts, connect your calendar in integrations (  ) and choose which meetings you'd like Fyxer to join in notetaker settings (  ).

You can also customize the background image of the notetaker, to control how others see it show up.

Best,

Archie

--

Archie Hollingsworth

Co-Founder | Fyxer
